items.title: Billions flowing out of bitcoin ETFs and private credit funds suggest rising market risks
items.description: Redemption requests in the $2 trillion private credit market surged to $15.6 billion in the second quarter, dwarfing bitcoin ETF outflows.

items.title: AI contracts, not bitcoin, now drive miner valuations, and Cipher and TeraWulf look cheap, says analyst
items.description: Compass Point analysts Michael Donovan and Ed Engel said markets are giving little credit to future AI data center pipelines despite billions of dollars in signed leases.

items.title: Ethereum's newest nonprofit wants to become Wall Street's guide to crypto
items.description: In this week's edition of The Protocol Newsletter, we’re digging into Ethereum Institutional, a new nonprofit aimed at educating financial institutions and banks about Ethereum.

items.title: Aave rolls out vaults for yield-hungry fintech investors
items.description: Aave's new Stable Vaults product lets wallets, exchanges and payment apps offer yields on stablecoin deposits

items.title: Age verification is the surveillance nobody voted for
items.description: KOSA and Chat Control both retreated on their most-criticized measures this week, but both kept mandatory age verification, the quiet mechanism that turns anonymous browsing into identified browsing for all of us, argues Billions CEO Evin McMullen.

items.title: Over $7.2 billion have migrated from LayerZero to Chainlink CCIP as Mantle joins exodus
items.description: Migrations to Chainlink CCIP so far include Kelp and Lombard, both of which brought over $1 billion, as well as Solv Protocol, Virtuals, Re and Kraken’s tokenized assets.

items.title: Pricing houses in bitcoin exposes dollar's loss of value
items.description: Your day-ahead look for July 9, 2026

items.title: Swift rolls out new blockchain ledger to bring 24/7 banking to 17 global giants
items.description: HSBC, UBS, Wells Fargo and Citi are among the 17 banks now preparing to pilot live transactions using tokenized digital assets on Swift’s new blockchain payments platform.

items.title: Bitcoin miners’ AI pivot faces investor scrutiny over insider sales
items.description: As AI-driven mining stocks retreat, investors are examining executive stock sales, governance and shareholder alignment across leading Bitcoin miners, according to Blocksbridge Consulting.

items.title: UK politicians mull permanent crypto donation ban in wake of Nigel Farage scandal
items.description: Labour lawmakers proposed a full moratorium on crypto donations in March, which some have sought be made permanent after millions of dollars in contributions were linked to Reform leader Nigel Farage.

items.title: Can Bitcoin hold $62K ahead of Friday’s $1.4 billion options expiry?
items.description: US 10-year Treasury yields nears dangerous level while major Deribit Bitcoin options expiry approaches.

items.title: MARA shares surge after 2 GW Texas infrastructure deal expands AI ambitions
items.description: The Bitcoin miner's shares climbed about 15% after announcing it's acquiring a Texas site with up to 2 GW of power capacity to expand its AI and digital infrastructure strategy.

items.title: Bitcoin traders reveal key levels as BTC price passes $63K after Trump Iran 'deal' comments
items.description: Bitcoin gained new upside targets for the daily close and beyond after US president Donald Trump said that Iran "wants to make a deal."

items.title: Bitcoin’s quantum dilemma: Bigger blocks or STARK proofs?
items.description: Dealing with large post quantum signatures slowing down Bitcoin can be solved with bigger blocks, or aggregating signatures using STARK proofs.

items.title: Hong Kong regulator orders new anti-phishing measures for crypto platforms
items.description: Hong Kong’s regulator has ordered crypto platforms and online brokers to meet newly issued phishing-resistant login requirements within the next 12 months.

items.title: Sony Bank gets US regulator nod to issue stablecoins
items.description: Sony Bank received preliminary approval from the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency to establish a US stablecoin issuance business, with $40 million in starting capital.

items.title: Meta jumps into AI coding market in effort to chase Anthropic and OpenAI
items.description: Meta is upgrading its Muse Spark artificial intelligence model under the leadership of AI chief Alexandr Wang.

items.title: June home sales disappoint as prices reach an all-time high
items.description: Home sales dropped in June month over month as mortgage rates remain stubbornly high. Prices hit an all-time high.

items.title: Alleged Reflecting Pool vandal David Hearn pleads not guilty; lawyer calls him 'scapegoat'
items.description: "If Mr. Hearn can be charged with a felony for touching the Reflecting Pool, every American is at risk," a lawyer said as he blasted the Trump administration.

items.title: Florida's Palm Beach airport renamed for Trump
items.description: Palm Beach International Airport is now called the Donald J. Trump International Airport, the FAA said Thursday.

items.title: Kalshi traders see roughly 50% odds of a rate hike in 2026 as Fed is split on policy
items.description: Wednesday's Fed minutes showed a divided outlook on where interest rates are headed this year. Traders on Kalshi see a 54% likelihood of a hike before 2027.

items.title: Anthropic appoints former Fed Chair Ben Bernanke to its independent trust
items.description: Members of the trust advise Anthropic leadership and do not hold any equity in the company.

items.title: Palo Alto CEO Arora says AI pricing needs to fall 90% as token costs skyrocket
items.description: Palo Alto Networks CEO Nikesh Arora said high token costs could prevent businesses from adopting artificial intelligence at scale.

items.title: These 10 states are best positioned to land AI data center deals despite rising public opposition
items.description: These ten U.S. states have the best infrastructure to win future AI data center deals despite rising public opposition.

items.title: Bitcoin little-changed at $62k as U.S.-Iran conflict re-escalates

US strikes Iranian railway bridges on route to city of Khamenei’s burial
items.description: Late supreme leader set to be interred in Mashhad on Thursday

items.title: There is no grand plan on Iran
items.description: An over-reaching Tehran seems to be looking at America’s confused moves and wondering what it may be missing

items.title: Iran’s new order takes shape after Khamenei burial
items.description: Ali Khamenei’s funeral leaves son Mojtaba — about whom Iranians know little — to confront enormous challenges facing the regime

items.title: The century-old device choking the world’s AI push
items.description: Surging data centre power demands are intensifying pressure on transformer supply chains

items.title: How much is AI hacking your emotions?
items.description: Disinformation and deepfakes are known risks but biased LLMs could also be used to influence public opinion

items.title: Around the World Cup in seven days
items.description: What it’s like living inside the world’s biggest football tournament

items.description: Ukraine drone strikes have hammered Russia’s refineries

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=x2eJlMatzK4! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: ‘I’ve no idea how they got her number’: My student-loan servicer called a friend after I missed a payment. Is that legal?
items.description: “She said it’s the second message they have left.”

items.title: Risky leveraged ETFs are booming in 2026. Some worry they could be making the stock market more volatile.
items.description: Some say traders who gravitate toward these products see big swings in the underlying stocks as a feature, not a bug.

items.title: Home prices hit new all-time high, deepening affordability woes
items.description: Home prices continue to climb even as buyers retreat from the market, according to fresh data from the National Association of Realtors.

items.title: Mortgage rates jump as tensions with Iran spook bond investors
items.description: The conflict between the U.S. and Iran is driving mortgage rates up, adding hundreds of dollars a month in extra costs for prospective home buyers.

items.title: Micron’s stock surges on multibillion-dollar U.S. manufacturing push
items.description: The memory-chip giant is reversing its recent slide after increasing its investment in the U.S. semiconductor supply chain.

items.title: This is why paper checks refuse to die
items.description: While some retailers still accept checks, an attempt to pay with anything other than cash or card is likely to result in a blank stare from a Gen Z cashier.

items.title: Only 5% of U.S. adults can ace this 8-question financial-literacy test. Can you?
items.description: Financial literacy just hit a 10-year low — and it is devastating bank accounts.

items.title: The dot-com crash was a $5 trillion blip. Why the next financial crisis could hit 4 times harder.
items.description: The next global financial crisis is already under way. Here is how it will unfold.

items.title: Is shock the new reality? | FT Rethink

1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. items.title: Gazans flock to World Cup screenings
items.description: Tournament provides welcome moments of respite as Palestinians follow progress of Arab teams

items.title: There is no grand plan on Iran
items.description: An over-reaching Tehran seems to be looking at America’s confused moves and wondering what it may be missing

items.title: Suspicious trading before 41% of takeovers sets new UK record
items.description: Annual FCA data shows abnormal trades hitting new highs amid deal frenzy for UK companies

items.title: Microsoft’s early AI lead has become a test of faith
items.description: The only certainty is that capital spending is going through the roof

items.title: Britain’s unsustainable mental health bill
items.description: Reducing spending on disability benefits is a major test for Andy Burnham

items.title: Ukrainian spy accused of killing Monaco hit woman shifts blame
items.description: Vladyslav Reut says accomplice pulled the trigger on the woman they hired to carry out a hit on a Ukrainian tycoon

items.title: Chemicals companies hit out at UK science funding cuts
items.description: Particle accelerator used by both public and private sector scientists is part of 15% research centre savings

items.title: Banijay picks London as new HQ for TV maker after $8bn merger
items.description: UK base for production group behind ‘The Traitors’ is another boost to country’s creative sector

items.title: Grocery Stores Lower Prices as Consumers Pare Spending
items.description: While shoppers may get better deals on some items, it’s unlikely their overall grocery bill will fall.

items.title: A $3.2 Trillion Deal-Making Frenzy Is Spurred by the A.I. Economy
items.description: This year’s boom includes the most spent on global deal-making in a six-month period in a decade. But questions persist about whether it can continue.

items.title: Renewed Fighting With Iran Shows Cracks in Peace-Trade Rally
items.description: Investors have taken heart from the fragile cease-fire between the United States and Iran before. Will they do so again?

items.title: Help! I Refueled My Rental Car, but Enterprise Charged Me Anyway.
items.description: A traveler to Germany returned his vehicle with slightly more gas than it had when he picked it up. So why was he billed $131 to fill up the tank?

items.title: Can Xenom Turn Working Out Into a Sport?
items.description: Xenom, a new fitness company, wants to make weekend warriors feel like pros.

items.title: The World Cup’s Biggest Match Is Nike Vs. Adidas
items.description: Second place in the hearts and wallets of Americans isn’t good enough for the world’s largest sportswear companies, who bet big on the tournament.

items.title: Oil Prices Remain Elevated Amid Shipping Traffic Slowdown in Strait of Hormuz
items.description: Fresh data showed that the number of ships moving through the Strait of Hormuz halved on Wednesday amid renewed fighting.

items.title: At Warsh’s First Fed Meeting, Some Officials Signaled Support for Raising Interest Rates
items.description: Minutes from Kevin M. Warsh’s first meeting as chairman showed heightened unease about inflation, which is running at a three-year high.
